function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/krbrelayx (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/impacket (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/ShadowCoerce (score=11); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_windows', 'ad'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['shell_windows', 'ad'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['shell_windows', 'ad'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use this tool when the engagement is in the 'any' phase against a target with '['shell_windows', 'ad']' as the attack surface (windows___ad, toolbox, github).
documentation.why_to_use: This tool is preferred because: Index entry in the Windows / AD category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
